     9→// distributed under the License is distributed on an "AS IS" BASIS,
    10→// WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.
    11→// See the License for the specific language governing permissions and
    12→// limitations under the License.
    13→
    14→package labels
    15→
    16→import (
    17→	"slices"
    18→	"strings"
    19→	"unicode"
    20→	"unicode/utf8"
    21→
    22→	"github.com/grafana/regexp"
    23→	"github.com/grafana/regexp/syntax"
    24→	"golang.org/x/text/unicode/norm"
    25→)
    26→
    27→const (
    28→	maxSetMatches = 256
    29→
    30→	// The minimum number of alternate values a regex should have to trigger
    31→	// the optimization done by optimizeEqualOrPrefixStringMatchers() and so use a map
    32→	// to match values instead of iterating over a list. This value has
    33→	// been computed running BenchmarkOptimizeEqualStringMatchers.
    34→	minEqualMultiStringMatcherMapThreshold = 16
    35→)
    36→
    37→type FastRegexMatcher struct {
    38→	// Under some conditions, re is nil because the expression is never parsed.
    39→	// We store the original string to be able to return it in GetRegexString().
    40→	reString string
    41→	re       *regexp.Regexp
    42→
    43→	setMatches    []string
    44→	stringMatcher StringMatcher
    45→	prefix        string
    46→	suffix        string
    47→	contains      []string
    48→
    49→	// matchString is the "compiled" function to run by MatchString().
    50→	matchString func(string) bool
    51→}
    52→
    53→func NewFastRegexMatcher(v string) (*FastRegexMatcher, error) {
    54→	m := &FastRegexMatcher{
    55→		reString: v,
    56→	}
    57→
    58→	m.stringMatcher, m.setMatches = optimizeAlternatingLiterals(v)
    59→	if m.stringMatcher != nil {
    60→		// If we already have a string matcher, we don't need to parse the regex
    61→		// or compile the matchString function. This also avoids the behavior in
    62→		// compileMatchStringFunction where it prefers to use setMatches when
    63→		// available, even if the string matcher is faster.
    64→		m.matchString = m.stringMatcher.Matches
    65→	} else {
    66→		parsed, err := syntax.Parse(v, syntax.Perl|syntax.DotNL)
    67→		if err != nil {
    68→			return nil, err
    69→		}
    70→
    71→		parsed = optimizeAlternatingSimpleContains(parsed)
    72→
    73→		m.re, err = regexp.Compile("^(?s:" + parsed.String() + ")$")
    74→		if err != nil {
    75→			return nil, err
    76→		}
    77→
    78→		// Remove any capture operations before trying to optimize the remaining operations.
    79→		clearCapture(parsed)
    80→
